name: Jamf Pro
description: Monitor Jamf Pro device management including computer enrollment, MDM compliance, and disk encryption status

config.setupInstructions:
1. Log in to Jamf Pro as an administrator.
2. Go to Settings > System > API Roles and Clients.
3. Create an API Role with 'Read Computers', 'Read Mobile Devices', and 'Read Computer Inventory Collection' permissions.
4. Create an API Client, assign the role, and copy the Client ID and Client Secret.
5. Run this command to get your access token:
   curl -X POST 'https://YOUR_DOMAIN/api/oauth/token' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -d 'grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET'
6. Copy the access_token value from the response and paste it below.
Note: Tokens expire — regenerate if you see authentication errors.

credentialFields.label: Jamf Pro URL
credentialFields.helpText: Your Jamf Pro server URL, e.g. company.jamfcloud.com (without https://)

credentialFields.label: Access Token
credentialFields.helpText: Bearer token obtained by exchanging your API client credentials. Run: curl -X POST 'https://YOUR_DOMAIN/api/oauth/token' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -d 'grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=CLIENT_ID&client_secret=CLIENT_SECRET'

checks.name: Secure Devices
checks.description: Verifies that computers are enrolled in Jamf Pro MDM management

checks.name: Device List
checks.description: Lists all mobile devices enrolled in Jamf Pro MDM

checks.name: Encryption at Rest
checks.description: Verifies that computers enrolled in Jamf Pro have FileVault/disk encryption enabled
